references.authors: Kaur S, Kular JS, Chandi RS
references.title: Effect of Temperature on Growth and Development of Thrips tabaci Lindeman in BT Cotton
references.journal: International Journal of Current Microbiology and Applied Sciences
references.institution: Department of Entomology, Punjab Agricultural University, Ludhiana, Punjab, India
values_informed: thermal_properties.optimal_temperature_range — growth index maximum at 30°C (7.92); highest thrips counts at 29.5-31°C mean temperature
upper_temperature_threshold 40°C — population declines at 40°C; survival drops to 34% at 35°C
egg.required_gdd — incubation period 2.39 days (35°C) to 4.58 days (25°C) on Bt cotton
larva.required_gdd — total nymphal period 5.10 days (35°C) to 9.93 days (25°C); 1st instar 2.29-4.23 days, 2nd instar 3.03-5.78 days
prepupa.required_gdd — pre-pupal period 1.28 days (35°C) to 2.12 days (25°C)
pupa.required_gdd — pupal period 1.85 days (35°C) to 3.21 days (25°C)
adult.required_gdd — total development period egg-to-adult 10.00 days (35°C) to 19.56 days (25°C)
adult longevity 8.21 days (35°C) to 22.19 days (25°C)
reproduction_model.reproduction_rate — fecundity-cum-viability 7.80 nymphs/female (35°C), 14.60 (30°C), 21.70 (25°C)
nymphal survival 34% at 35°C, 63% at 30°C, 78% at 25°C — survival declines sharply with temperature rise
yield loss potential 30-50% (Cook et al. 2011, cited in paper)

references.authors: Ahmed MH, Ullah MI, Raza ABM, Afzal M, Khaliq A, Iftikhar Y, Aatif HM
references.title: Population Dynamics of Thrips tabaci (Lindeman) in Relation to Abiotic Climate Factors on Bt and Non-Bt Cotton Cultivars
references.institution: Department of Entomology, University of Sargodha, Sargodha, Pakistan
values_informed: thermal_properties.optimal_temperature_range — highest thrips counts at max temperature 34-35°C; mean temp 29.5-31°C gave highest population
upper_temperature_threshold — population declined at 40°C
humidity_preferences.optimal_humidity_range — maximum thrips population in range 75-85% RH; positive correlation with humidity r=0.40**
suitability_weights.temperature 0.55 — minimum temperature showed highest significant correlation with thrips population r=0.57**
suitability_weights.rainfall 0.2 — thrips and mites favor low rainfall for development
crop_stage_modifiers.seedling 1.2 and vegetative 1.1 — population peaks at 8th-9th sampling interval (August) then declines; highest on upper canopy
active season June-October in Punjab Pakistan field conditions
peak population 13 thrips/leaf (Bt-3701); Bt variety FH-113 showed lowest thrips population
correlation matrix: thrips vs min temp r=0.57**, thrips vs humidity r=0.40**, thrips vs avg temp r=0.48**; max temp correlation non-significant

references.authors: Joseph SV, Braman K, Hudson W, Nair S
references.title: Biology and Management of Thrips Affecting the Production Nursery and Landscape
references.institution: Department of Entomology, University of Georgia
values_informed: lifecycle_model structure — 6 stages confirmed: egg, 1st instar larva (foliage), 2nd instar larva (foliage), prepupa (soil/leaf litter), pupa (soil/leaf litter), adult (foliage)
prepupa and pupa economic_damage_weight 0.0 — pupal stages are resting stages and do not feed
larva.mobility low and adult.mobility moderate — larvae wingless and weak; adults winged but generally weak fliers, dispersed by wind
life cycle completed in ~2 weeks under favorable conditions; multiple generations per year
damage_profile.crop_damage_types — rasping feeding causes silver/bronze colored speckles; leaf distortion, premature drying and shedding of buds and flowers

intervention_sensitivity.biological_control_sensitivity moderate — natural enemies include lacewings, spiders, predatory thrips, mites, minute pirate bugs; Thripobius semiluteus wasp specific to greenhouse thrips
intervention_sensitivity.treatment_resistance_risk moderate — continuous insecticide exposure leads to resistance development; thrips hide in plant crevices limiting chemical reach
monitoring: yellow and blue sticky cards effective; blue/white water pan traps also used
